articles.title: Scientists say travel could slow aging and boost your health
articles.summary: A new study suggests travel could be a surprisingly powerful anti-aging tool. By viewing tourism through the lens of entropy, researchers found that positive travel experiences may help the body stay balanced and resilient. Activities like exploring new places, staying active, and connecting with others can boost immunity, metabolism, and stress recovery. However, stressful or unsafe travel could reverse these benefits.

articles.title: A 75-million-year-old fossil reveals a shocking tyrannosaur secret
articles.summary: Tyrannosaurs may be famous as fearsome apex predators, but new research reveals a more opportunistic—and slightly grim—side to their behavior. Using high-resolution 3D scans, a researcher identified precise bite marks on a massive tyrannosaur foot bone, showing that a smaller tyrannosaur had fed on the remains of a much larger relative over 75 million years ago.

articles.title: This tiny outer Solar System world has an atmosphere. It shouldn’t
articles.summary: Astronomers have spotted something surprising in the far outer Solar System—a faint, short-lived atmosphere clinging to a tiny icy world that shouldn’t be able to hold one at all. The object, called 2002 XV93, is far smaller than Pluto, yet observations during a rare stellar alignment revealed its presence through a subtle dimming of starlight. Even more puzzling, calculations suggest this atmosphere should vanish within about 1,000 years unless it’s constantly being replenished.

articles.title: This simple amino acid supplement greatly reduces Alzheimer’s damage
articles.summary: A new study suggests a surprisingly simple compound could help fight Alzheimer’s disease. Researchers found that arginine—an inexpensive amino acid already considered safe—can reduce the buildup of toxic amyloid proteins in the brain, a hallmark of the disease. In animal models, oral arginine not only lowered harmful protein deposits but also improved behavior and reduced brain inflammation.

articles.title: The da Vinci bloodline is unlocking the genius’s genetic secrets
articles.summary: After centuries of mystery, scientists are edging closer to uncovering Leonardo da Vinci’s biological secrets. A massive 30-year effort has mapped his family across 21 generations, identified living male descendants, and even confirmed shared DNA markers that stretch back to the Renaissance. Meanwhile, excavations at a family tomb in Vinci could provide ancient remains for comparison, raising the possibility of reconstructing Leonardo’s genetic profile.

Scientists say warming temperatures are supercharging these episodes beyond natural climate patterns.

Some texts reveal correspondence between rulers, while others show the rise of complex bureaucracies. One tablet even records something as ordinary—and relatable—as a receipt for beer.

Теперь NASA готовится к миссии Artemis III и дальнейшим исследованиям Луны, устраняя лишь незначительные технические вопросы.
articles.original_description: Artemis II proved NASA’s deep space systems are ready for the next leap. Orion survived its high-speed return with improved heat shield performance and pinpoint landing accuracy, while the SLS rocket nailed its trajectory. Even the launch pad upgrades paid off, with minimal damage despite the powerful liftoff. With only minor issues to resolve, NASA is now gearing up for Artemis III and future Moon missions.

articles.title: NASA shuts down 49-year-old Voyager 1 instrument to keep it alive
articles.summary: Voyager 1 just powered down a nearly 50-year-old instrument to stay alive in deep space. Despite the shutdown, it continues to send back unique data from beyond our solar system. Engineers are now working on a bold plan that could extend its life — and possibly revive the instrument later.

articles.title: Scientists reveal creatine’s hidden power beyond muscle gains
articles.summary: Creatine might be famous in the gym, but its real story is far more interesting. Naturally produced in the body, it helps power cells by rapidly regenerating ATP—the fuel that keeps muscles, the brain, and even the heart running during intense activity. Supplementing with creatine can boost short bursts of physical performance and may even support memory, mood, and cognitive speed, especially in people with lower baseline levels.

articles.title: Mexico moves to combat pollution following Guardian investigations
articles.summary: В ответ на расследования о загрязнении окружающей среды в районе завода, перерабатывающего токсичные отходы из США, правительство Мексики объявило о комплексных мерах по борьбе с промышленным загрязнением.  Эти меры включают штрафы в размере 4,8 млн долларов для предприятия и внедрение новой системы мониторинга качества воздуха.
articles.original_description:
After stories revealed high levels of contamination in neighborhood around factory processing US toxic waste, government announces sweeping array of tactics

The Mexican government has announced it will pursue a sweeping array of tactics to combat industrial pollution, from $4.8m in fines against a plant processing US hazardous waste to the rollout of a new industrial air-monitoring system, following investigations by the Guardian and Quinto Elemento Lab, a Mexican investigative unit.
